steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The model prescribes a single uniform methane mole fraction because observational data are too sparse and discrepant to determine Titan's methane levels precisely. We need the choice that follows logically from this deliberate simplification.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "even though the model's outputs sometimes agree with observational data, Titan's real methane mole fraction is likely higher than the methane mole fraction used in the model.": The passage gives no basis for predicting the direction of any error — only that uncertainty exists. ✗
  • "inconsistencies across the model's simulations of Titan's precipitation and humidity could be attributable to variations in the moon's methane mole fraction.": The passage is concerned with how the model compares to real observations, not with internal model consistency. ✗
  • "further observations of Titan may clarify the moon's methane mole fraction sufficiently for the model to employ a single value rather than a range.": Better data would actually improve the model, not eliminate the need for it. ✗
  • "some disagreements between the model's simulations of Titan's precipitation and humidity and the moon's actual precipitation and humidity are to be expected.": A model that reduces uncertain, variable reality to a single fixed value will necessarily diverge from some real measurements — those disagreements are an expected consequence of the simplification. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
